title: trifolium pratense subsp. pallidum
intro: Trifolium pratense subsp. pallidum, also known as white clover, is a perennial legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It is a subspecies of the red clover plant and is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of white clover include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of white clover, including teas, infusions, and capsules, have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues, boost immune function, and promote overall well-being, making it a valuable addition to natural health remedies.
